subtasks.description: Evaluate bulk PII export request against data governance and privacy policies. Verify requestor authorization, assess compliance with data protection regulations (GDPR, CCPA, etc.), determine if data masking/anonymization is required, and issue approval or denial with justification.

plan.cost_rationale: This is explicitly a governance and compliance decision, not a data retrieval task. Bulk export of customer PII (names, emails, phone numbers) requires policy evaluation against data protection regulations, access control rules, and organizational governance frameworks. A mid-tier policy_action agent is appropriate because it must evaluate multiple policy dimensions (GDPR/CCPA compliance, requestor role authorization, data residency rules, audit logging requirements) and potentially issue conditional approval (e.g., approval with mandatory anonymization or role-based filtering). No sql_gen is invoked until policy clearance is established. This prevents unauthorized PII exposure and ensures compliance-first workflow design.

output.reason: Bulk PII export by a data-analyst role lacks required Legal/CISO approvals (POL-501) and violates data minimization since names, emails, and phone numbers are sensitive attributes without stated business-critical justification (POL-502). Audit logging and retention controls (POL-503) can be applied conditionally but do not override missing approvals.
applied_constraints: Provide business justification or apply anonymization/masking
Enable audit logging with 30-day retention if approved
